preexisting_diverged_annotation.claim: DIVERGED: (language-forced) Rust-specific re-wake. The base `DialogPrivateEngine::Cycle` body runs BEFORE on_cycle_ext (Phase-3.6 Task 2 ordering). State mutations this closure makes — setting `dlg.pending_result`, pushing `pending_actions` — are not visible to the base body this Cycle. We return `true` on any mutation to keep the engine awake so the next Cycle's base body observes them. C++ doesn't need this: `Finish(POSITIVE)` in emFileDialog::Cycle finalizes via same-call-stack re-entry into emDialog::Cycle's finalize path.

rows.notes: IsSignaled site: crates/emcore/src/emFileDialog.rs:169 `if ctx.IsSignaled(od_sig) ...`. Also a fallback `scheduler.connect(od_finish_sig, outer_engine_id)` at emFileDialog.rs:733 in the post-show subscription path. Subscribe-react pair complete.

Rust has no virtual base-class inheritance; the `emModel` context-registry infrastructure is not yet ported, so callers receive an explicit `Rc<RefCell<Self>>` and manage sharing themselves. The observable loading contract — async engine fires on schedule, signals on completion — is preserved.

rust_evidence.snippet: fn Cycle: 'if self.needs_update { self.needs_update = false; self.update_children(ctx); }' — needs_update set by notice() on NF_VIEWING_CHANGED, not by model change signal. update_children re-reads model.GetItemRecs() each call but no signal subscription wires the model to the panel.

rows.notes: emVirtualCosmosModel exposes no GetChangeSignal accessor and never fires one (it has no signal at all). Panel reacts to viewport changes (NF_VIEWING_CHANGED), not to model changes — observably equivalent for static cosmos but breaks if the model ever reloads while the panel is viewed.

rust_evidence.snippet: pub fn GetChangeSignal(&self) -> u64 — returns a generation counter, not a SignalId; consumers cannot subscribe via AddWakeUpSignal/IsSignaled.

rows.notes: C++ emFileManViewConfig::GetChangeSignal() returns const emSignal&. Rust returns u64 generation; panels poll this via cached_change_gen rather than subscribing. Fixing requires allocating a SignalId in emFileManViewConfig and firing it on apply().

rust_evidence.snippet: pub fn GetCommandsSignal(&self) -> u64 — returns generation counter, not a SignalId.

rows.notes: C++ returns const emSignal&. Rust returns generation u64; consumer polls. Fix: allocate SignalId for the commands change set.

rust_evidence.snippet: pub fn GetSelectionSignal(&self) -> u64 — returns generation counter, not a SignalId.

rows.notes: C++ returns const emSignal&. Rust returns generation u64; consumers compare cached generation to live value.

rust_evidence.snippet: No GetChangeSignal accessor exposed. emFileLinkModel only implements GetFileStateSignal (line 265 — private trait impl). C++ emFileLinkModel inherits emStructRec::GetChangeSignal; Rust port has no equivalent.

rows.notes: emFileLinkPanel.Model, emAutoplay.Model, emVirtualCosmos.Model all reference an emStructRec-derived model. Rust emRec hierarchy lacks the change-signal exposure. Fix requires propagating GetChangeSignal through the rec model layer (or per-model wrapping). The four consumer rows are co-listed; "Model" semantically resolves to emFileLinkModel for emFileLinkPanel-56/72, emAutoplayViewModel for emAutoplay-1171 (see sibling row emAutoplayViewModel-accessor-model-change), and emVirtualCosmosModel for emVirtualCosmos-575 (see sibling row).

rust_evidence.snippet: pub struct emAutoplayViewModel (emAutoplay.rs:800-) defines no GetChangeSignal accessor. Note: emAutoplay.rs:129 has emAutoplayConfig::GetChangeSignal — that is the config rec, not the view-model. C++ emAutoplayViewModel::GetChangeSignal (emAutoplay.h:221) is missing.

rows.notes: emAutoplay-1171 panel references the view-model; the config-side accessor at line 129 is a different object.

rust_evidence.snippet: pub struct emAutoplayViewModel defines no GetProgressSignal accessor. C++ emAutoplayViewModel::GetProgressSignal (emAutoplay.h:241) absent in port.

rows.notes: Renamed from -accessor-model-state per B-003 brainstorm 703fa462; C++ second signal is ProgressSignal (emAutoplay.h:241,279,318; cpp:911), not a state signal.

rust_evidence.snippet: pub struct emVirtualCosmosModel (emVirtualCosmos.rs:213) lacks GetChangeSignal. Cycle at line 826 polls notice flag instead of subscribing.

emFilePanel exposes pub fn GetVirFileState() -> VirtualFileState (line 100) but no GetVirFileStateSignal() accessor. C++ emFilePanel::GetVirFileStateSignal (emFilePanel.h) returns const emSignal&. Subclasses cannot subscribe; they recompute via Cycle.

rows.notes: emFilePanel-derived panels (image, dir, dirstat, filelink, stocksfile) all subscribe to this in C++ to react to VFS state changes. In Rust they instead poll vir_file_state in their Cycle.

rust_evidence.snippet: pub struct emStocksFileModel defines no GetChangeSignal accessor (no SignalId field, no fn GetChangeSignal). C++ emStocksFileModel inherits emStructRec::GetChangeSignal; Rust port has no equivalent surface.
emStocksConfig defines no GetChangeSignal accessor. C++ emStocksConfig inherits emStructRec::GetChangeSignal.
pub struct emStocksPricesFetcher (emStocksPricesFetcher.rs:18-) defines no GetChangeSignal accessor. C++ emStocksPricesFetcher::GetChangeSignal returns const emSignal&; Rust port omits it.

emFileModel::Cycle (line 483) does not test IsSignaled(update_signal). C++ AddWakeUpSignal/RemoveWakeUpSignal toggle (emFileModel.cpp:96-105) based on ignore_update_signal is not implemented. AcquireUpdateSignalModel is exposed (line 343) but the model does not subscribe to itself.

rows.notes: Model-internal: emFileModel subscribes to its shared UpdateSignalModel to react to external updates. Rust accessor exists but the wake-up subscription itself is absent.

reconciliation.note: B-007 design corrects a latent semantic mis-port: AcquireUpdateSignalModel at emFileModel.rs:343 was returning a dead per-model signal; design re-wires to the shared App::file_update_signal broadcast. Bug fix, not annotated DIVERGED.

rust_evidence.snippet: pub fn CheckSaveTimer(&mut self) -> bool { if let Some(deadline) = self.save_timer_deadline ... } — uses std::time::Instant deadline plus caller-driven CheckSaveTimer instead of subscribing to a timer signal.

rows.notes: Model-internal: file 18 comment notes emTimer::TimerCentral is unported. Save timer simulated via Instant; no SignalId allocated.

cleanup_items.full_block:
// DIVERGED: (language-forced) C++ uses AddWakeUpSignal / IsSignaled. Rust uses Rc<Cell<bool>>
// flags set by on_click callbacks and polled in Cycle.
